_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Doc-generation job observability + execution report + dispatch fix

steps.description: Add `report JSONB NULL` column to doc_generation_jobs via a new Alembic revision; add the corresponding `report: Mapped[dict[str, Any] | None]` field to DocGenerationJob in orch/db/models.py. Migration must be reversible (downgrade drops the column). No data backfill. No index on the column.
Review S01: migration safety (additive, nullable, no default), downgrade correctness, ORM mapping matches DB type, naming follows project convention, no existing rows broken.

Dispatch unit. (a) Fix `_build_agent_command` opencode branch in orch/daemon/doc_job_poller.py: replace `/execute {job.id}` with `/doc-job {job.id}`. Plumb the rendered command string back through `_launch_job` so it can later flow into complete_doc_job's `command_issued` kwarg (S05 will consume it). (b) Create commands/doc-job.md (frontmatter `description` + `agent`, prose body) routing to iw-doc-generator or iw-doc-system per editorial-category selection. (c) Add new CLI command `iw doc-job-status <job-id> [--json]` in orch/cli/doc_commands.py (and register in orch/cli/main.py). Returns the DocGenerationJob row joined with the ProjectDoc context the agent needs: project_id, doc_id, doc title, editorial_category, section_guides_snapshot, guide_snapshot, status. JSON output mode mirrors `iw item-status --json`. Read-only — never mutates state. (d) Add a 'Job lifecycle (when invoked via /doc-job)' section to skills/iw-doc-generator/SKILL.md and skills/iw-doc-system/SKILL.md documenting `iw doc-job-status` for context, `iw doc-update` for content, `iw doc-job-done` (or `--error`) for closure. The two SKILL.md sections are character-for-character identical.

Review S03 (dispatch unit): opencode dispatch string is now `/doc-job <id>` (not `/execute`); claude-code branch unchanged; rendered command string captured for downstream use; commands/doc-job.md frontmatter shape matches commands/execute.md; new `iw doc-job-status` CLI is read-only and returns all context fields the skill needs; both SKILL.md updates are identical and additive (existing content untouched).

Observability unit. (a) PID liveness probe in DocJobPoller.poll: before the wall-clock stall sweep, mark any running job with a dead agent_pid as failed via complete_doc_job(error='agent process exited without calling iw doc-job-done', worktree_path=...). Skip jobs younger than ~10s (race protection). (b) New orch/doc_report.py module with pure helpers: read_log_tail, parse_tool_calls, count_doc_update_invocations, build_execution_report. Factor strip_ansi into orch/utils/log_capture.py and re-import (no inline duplication). (c) Modify DocService.complete_doc_job to accept keyword-only kwargs `worktree_path`, `cli_tool`, `command_issued`. Read <worktree>/ai-dev/logs/doc_job_<job.id>.log, write the last 64 KB (with truncation marker) to job.agent_output, and write the full execution report dict to job.report. Both the poller's stall path AND the agent-driven `iw doc-job-done` path must populate these fields. Method remains idempotent on terminal state. (d) Add `report` to JobsAggregator._build_doc_generation_raw so the dashboard can render it.

Review S05 (observability unit): PID-liveness probe ordering in poll() (runs before stall sweep), no race vs. agent_pid being None, helper modularity (orch/doc_report.py), 64 KB truncation marker text, complete_doc_job idempotency, error-string-to-outcome classification matches probe call site, ANSI strip lives in exactly one location, aggregator surfaces `report`.

Add three endpoints to dashboard/routers/docs.py (or jobs_ui.py if more natural — pick one and stick to it): GET /project/{pid}/jobs/doc_generation/{job_id}/log/tail returning {lines, truncated_from_bytes, file_size_bytes} with last 200 ANSI-stripped lines (cap configurable via ?n=, hard max 1000); GET .../log/stream returning text/event-stream with one log line per `data:` event, periodic `event:ping` heartbeat every 15s, sentinel `event:status data:terminal` then close on terminal status; GET .../log/raw returning text/plain with Content-Disposition: attachment and the unmodified original log content. Resolve file path from Project.repo_root + ai-dev/logs/doc_job_<job.id>.log. Return 404 with explanatory body when file is missing. No path traversal. Reject non-doc_generation job_ids.

Review S07: route registration order, file-path resolution + path traversal protection, SSE event shape and close semantics, 404 vs 500 mapping, no DB calls in the SSE generator after first lookup (avoid holding a session open for the stream's lifetime), heartbeat interval, content-type / charset on each endpoint.

Update dashboard/templates/pages/project/job_detail.html, under the existing `job.job_type.value == 'doc_generation'` branch: (a) Live Log card visible while job.status == 'running' — uses htmx SSE extension `<div hx-ext="sse" sse-connect=".../log/stream" sse-swap="message">` writing into a scrollable monospace `<pre>`; (b) Execution Report card visible whenever `raw.get('report')` is truthy — outcome pill, duration, command_issued (in `<code>`), tool-call summary table (tool / exit_code), doc_update_invocations count, lint_warning_count, diagnosis line; (c) 'Download raw log' link to /log/raw whenever the file exists (server passes a `log_file_exists` flag in the template context — coordinate with S07). Use existing Tailwind classes; do not invent new ones (avoid JIT-purge issues per dashboard/CLAUDE.md). Run `make css` after editing if new classes are needed.

Review S09: template correctness, htmx SSE wiring, graceful degradation when SSE unavailable, accessibility (sr-only labels for the log region), no inline scripts, conditional rendering correct for queued/running/completed/failed states, 'Download raw log' link only shown when the file exists, Tailwind classes do not break JIT purging.

Unit tests: tests/unit/test_doc_job_poller_pid_liveness.py (mock os.kill); tests/unit/test_doc_report.py (replay tests/fixtures/doc_jobs/doc_00004_replay.log; assert outcome=failed_process_exited, doc_update_invocations=0, wrong-dispatch diagnosis; success and timeout fixtures); tests/unit/test_doc_service_complete_writes_output.py (truncation rule, AC4 schema, idempotency, missing-log handling); tests/unit/test_doc_job_status_cli.py (new CLI command — JSON shape, joins ProjectDoc context, read-only). Integration tests: tests/integration/test_doc_job_log_endpoints.py (testcontainer per tests/CLAUDE.md; fixture log file; /log/tail / /log/stream / /log/raw assertions; missing-file → 404). Capture the actual DOC-00004 log into tests/fixtures/doc_jobs/doc_00004_replay.log.

Review S11: tests are falsifiable on main (would fail before this CR's changes); semantic-correctness assertions, not shape-only; no live DB connection per tests/CLAUDE.md; no `importlib.reload(orch.config)`; psycopg URL is rewritten in testcontainers; no flaky time-based assertions in the SSE test (use a tight upper bound, not a sleep+assert); fixtures clean up tmp files.

Cross-step review: integration coherence S01 schema → S03 dispatch + new CLI → S05 writers → S07 readers → S09 renderers → S11 tests; aggregator's `report` field flows through to the template; PID-liveness path produces a report whose outcome=failed_process_exited; dispatch fix and skill updates align (slash command name and lifecycle calls match — including `iw doc-job-status`); end-to-end: a hypothetical successful run produces agent_output, report, AND `/log/stream` closes correctly; full unit + integration suite passes locally.
QV: Linting
QV: Formatting
QV: Type checking
QV: Architecture
QV: Security SAST
QV: Unit tests
QV: Integration tests
QV: Browser verification — confirm Execution Report card renders, Captured log fallback shows historical content, Download raw log link works, no regressions on adjacent job pages
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
